nativeRange.habitat: Streambanks, pond margins, wet meadows, and marshes

summary: A versatile North American stem plant with bright green leaves and subtle purple undersides; easy to grow in moderate light and beginner-friendly community tanks.
careNotes: Lobelia cardinalis is a hardy stem plant native to North American waterways. In its submerged aquarium form the leaves are light green and compact (10–20 cm), while the emersed form develops purple leaf undersides and striking scarlet flowers. Regular trimming promotes bushy lateral shoot growth. The plant requires both macro- and micro-nutrient fertilisation; liquid fertilisers are generally more effective than root tabs alone. It tolerates a wide temperature range (16–26 °C) and is one of the few plants that grows well at cooler temperatures. Lighting note: Buce Plant (primary source) lists medium–high light demand while Tropica lists it as low demand; the conservative midpoint (medium) is used here. CO2 note: Buce Plant recommends CO2 injection; Tropica rates it as low-demand (optional); CO2 is marked optional to reflect the more conservative consensus. The plant is tolerant of most peaceful tankmates and works well in shrimp tanks. Contains alkaloids — not toxic to fish or invertebrates at normal plant densities but should not be ingested in large quantities. Taxonomy note: Lobeliaceae (listed by Buce Plant) is now treated as a subfamily of Campanulaceae; Campanulaceae is used here.
breedingNotes: Propagated by trimming mature stems and replanting cuttings in the substrate. Lateral shoots that develop near the stem base can also be separated and replanted. When grown emersed, seeds can be collected from the red flowers for propagation.

additional.name: Tropica
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing (10–30 cm, consistent) and care level. Tropica rates lighting as low-demand vs. Buce Plant's medium–high; conservative medium used. Tropica rates CO2 as low-demand (optional) vs. Buce Plant's recommended; optional used. Note: the manifest-supplied Tropica URL (plantdetails/4471/4471) returned data for a different species (Limnophila aquatica); the correct Tropica page (4494/4494) was found via site search and used instead.

additional.name: Shrimp and Snail Breeder (aquariumbreeder.com)
additional.notes: Used for temperature range (16–26 °C), gH (2–12), kH (2–10), native range countries, and compatibility details. Data broadly consistent with primary source.
